2603.18557.title: Cross-Lingual LLM-Judge Transfer via Evaluation Decomposition
2603.18557.paper_summary: As large language models are increasingly deployed across diverse real-world applications, extending automated evaluation beyond English has become a critical challenge. Existing evaluation approaches are predominantly English-focused, and adapting them to other languages is hindered by the scarcity and cost of human-annotated judgments in most languages. We introduce a decomposition-based evaluation framework built around a Universal Criteria Set (UCS). UCS consists of a shared, language-agnostic set of evaluation dimensions, producing an interpretable intermediate representation that supports cross-lingual transfer with minimal supervision. Experiments on multiple faithfulness tasks across languages and model backbones demonstrate consistent improvements over strong baselines without requiring target-language annotations.
2603.18557.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型在各種現實應用中的日益普及，將自動評估擴展到英語以外的語言已成為一項關鍵挑戰。現有的評估方法主要集中在英語，而將其適應到其他語言的過程受到大多數語言中人類標註判斷的稀缺性和成本的阻礙。我們介紹了一個基於分解的評估框架，該框架圍繞著一個通用標準集（UCS）構建。UCS由一組共享的、與語言無關的評估維度組成，生成一個可解釋的中間表示，支持以最小的監督進行跨語言轉移。在多種語言和模型骨幹上的多個忠實度任務實驗中，顯示出相對於強基線的一致性改進，而無需目標語言的標註。
2603.18557.author: Ivaxi Sheth et.al.
2603.18557.authors: Ivaxi Sheth, Zeno Jonke, Amin Mantrach, Saab Mansour
